_comment: Labeled calibration set for the LLM evaluator. Each case gives the evaluator a self-contained artifact and a single assertion, with the known-correct verdict. The artifact is passed inline so the environment is fully deterministic — any run-to-run variance is the judge's, which is exactly what we measure. Cases are tagged by kind: 'deterministic' should be rock-solid; 'subjective' needs judgment; 'borderline' is intentionally near the decision boundary to surface flakiness.

cases.name: det-contains-pass
cases.assertion: The output contains a line where status equals ok
cases.artifact:
starting service...
status: ok
listening on :8080

cases.name: det-exit-fail
cases.assertion: The exit code reported in the log is 0
cases.artifact:
running migration 0042...
ERROR: relation "users" already exists
exit code: 1

cases.name: det-count-pass
cases.assertion: Exactly three items are listed under dependencies
cases.artifact:
dependencies:
  - requests
  - click
  - rich
dev-dependencies:
  - pytest

cases.name: det-count-fail
cases.assertion: Exactly three items are listed under dependencies

cases.name: subj-commit-pass
cases.assertion: The commit message body explains why the change was made, not only what changed
cases.artifact:
Cap upload retries at 3

Unbounded retries were holding connections open during backend
outages and exhausting the pool. Capping at 3 with backoff frees
connections while still riding out brief blips.

cases.name: subj-commit-fail
cases.assertion: The commit message body explains why the change was made, not only what changed
cases.artifact:
fix stuff

changed the retry loop and updated a few things

cases.name: border-partial-sections
cases.assertion: All three required sections are present: Installation, Usage, and License
cases.artifact:
# My Tool

## Installation
run npm install

## Usage
run the thing

cases.name: border-todo-present
cases.assertion: There are no TODO or FIXME placeholders in the code

cases.name: border-exact-body
cases.assertion: The response body is exactly {"status":"ok"} with no extra fields

cases.name: border-descriptive-name
cases.assertion: The function name clearly describes what the function does
cases.artifact:
def proc(d):
    return [x * 2 for x in d if x > 0]
